page: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by the QEMU hypervisor, featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. The processor configuration delivers 12 physical cores with one thread per core, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is set at 23.0 GB, which translates to approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 480 GB SSD, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the cpx52 demonstrates strong single-core performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core scores. Memory subsystem performance is also notable, with large-block read and write bandwidths and the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. The instance also shows strong capabilities in low-parameter LLM inference, specifically for 135M and 7B models, while maintaining average throughput for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cpx52 offers a high density of vCPUs and local SSD storage relative to its resource class, though this is balanced by the shared CPU allocation which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads. The lack of dedicated public IPv4 addresses and GPU acceleration are additional tradeoffs to consider. This server is well-suited for workloads such as lightweight web serving, text and image processing, software development environments, and small-scale database operations where single-core burst capability is prioritized over sustained, dedicated multi-core execution.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, 23.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 480 GB local SSD. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance provides a resource ratio of approximately 1.95 GB of memory per core. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and high large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average multi-core and database processing capabilities. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded workloads, the server offers high resource density for its class. It is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and low-parameter language model inference where single-core burst performance is highly valued.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 is an x86_64 virtual server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 23 GB RAM, and a 480 GB SSD. It offers strong single-core performance and is suited for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx52 features 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 23 GB RAM, and a 480 GB SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory benchmark performance.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server featuring twelve AMD EPYC cores and bundled SSD storage, optimized for strong single-core execution.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
23.0 GB of system memory and 480 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation suitable for bursty, non-continuous workloads
Well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and text processing
